basic_details.department: Gujarat Maritime Board (GMB), under the Ports and Transport Department
basic_details.short_information: GSSSB English Stenographer Recruitment 2026 has been announced by the Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) for 1 vacancy of English Stenographer, Grade-2, Class-3 under the Gujarat Maritime Board (GMB), which functions under the Ports and Transport Department. Online applications can be submitted through the OJAS website from 15 July 2026 (2:00 PM) to 29 July 2026 (11:59 PM). This article covers the important dates, vacancy details, eligibility criteria, pay scale, application fee, selection process and the step-by-step online application procedure for this recruitment.

important_dates.raw: Online Application Start Date: 15 July 2026 (2:00 PM) Online Application Last Date: 29 July 2026 (11:59 PM) Last Date to Pay Examination Fee: 1 August 2026 (11:59 PM) Reference Date for Age, Educational Qualification and Other Eligibility: 29 July 2026 Probable Qualifying Test (Shorthand Test) Month: September 2026 (tentative, as per the notification) | The exact examination date, time and admit card download instructions will be published later on the GSSSB website. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official website regularly for updates.

application_fee.details: The fee must be paid only online through Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI or Wallet, on or before 1 August 2026 (11:59 PM). A transaction fee will apply in addition to the examination fee. The fee paid is refundable only to those candidates who actually appear for the examination; it will not be refunded to candidates who do not appear.

age_limit.details: As on 29 July 2026, the candidate's age must not be less than 20 years and not more than 40 years. The upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates already in Gujarat Government service, as per the applicable rules. Age relaxation for other categories is as follows: | Category Relaxation Maximum Age Limit General category women candidates 5 years 45 years Reserved category male candidates 5 years 45 years Reserved category women candidates 10 years (5+5) 45 years General category disabled male candidates 10 years 45 years General category disabled women candidates 15 years (10+5) 45 years Reserved category disabled male candidates 15 years (5+10) 45 years Reserved category disabled women candidates 20 years (5+10+5) 45 years Ex-servicemen Duration of service plus 3 years As applicable

qualification.details: The candidate must be a citizen of India or possess the nationality prescribed under Rule-7 of the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. | A bachelor's degree from any university established or incorporated by or under a Central or State Act in India, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Government. Basic knowledge of computer applications as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi, or both. | Candidates who do not currently hold a certificate for basic computer knowledge can still apply, but they must produce such a certificate before appointment; otherwise they will not be eligible for appointment. | As on 29 July 2026, the candidate's age must not be less than 20 years and not more than 40 years. The upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates already in Gujarat Government service, as per the applicable rules. Age relaxation for other categories is as follows:

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be appointed on a fixed monthly pay of Rs. 40,800/- for the first five years, along with other benefits admissible under the relevant Finance Department resolutions. On satisfactory completion of five years of service, the candidate becomes eligible for regular appointment in the 7th Pay Commission pay scale of Rs. 35,400/- to Rs. 1,12,400/- (Level-6), subject to the outcome of SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012 pending before the Supreme Court.

selection_process: Topic Type Marks
Essay Writing Descriptive 10
Precis Writing Descriptive 10
Letter Writing (2 letters) Descriptive 20
Translation from Gujarati to English Descriptive 10
Translation from English to Gujarati Descriptive 10
English Grammar Objective 20
Basic Computer Knowledge/Operations Objective 20
The selection process for the English Stenographer
Grade-2 post consists of the following stages:
Dictation of an English passage in shorthand at a speed of 80 words per minute for two minutes
followed by transcription at a speed of 7 words per minute (in legible handwriting
not typing). This test checks the candidate's shorthand-writing ability; only candidates who meet the required skill level are declared eligible for the Main Examination.
Paper-1: English Language Proficiency and Computer Knowledge (Descriptive and Objective) - 100 Marks
2 Hours
The Basic Computer Knowledge portion (20 marks) covers computer basics
software (MS Word
MS Excel
MS PowerPoint
MS Outlook
Windows
Indic)
working with internet and email
and basics of networking and cyber security.
Paper-2: Skill Test (Shorthand and Transcription Typing) - 100 Marks
Dictation of 2 passages of 4 minutes each (8 minutes total) at a shorthand speed of 100 words per minute in English. Transcription/typing time: 50 minutes
done on a computer. For every 3 errors in transcription
1 mark will be deducted from the allotted marks. Minimum qualifying marks for Paper-2 is 50; marks below 50 are treated as zero.
The minimum qualifying marks for Paper-1 is 40 and for Paper-2 is 50. The final merit list is prepared out of the total 200 marks from both papers
and there will be no waiting list for this recruitment.
how_to_apply: Click on "Online Application" and select GSSSB.
Select Advertisement No. 436/2026-27 (English Stenographer, Grade-2, Class-3) and click Apply.
Click "Apply Now," then "Skip" to open the application form, and fill in the Personal Details (fields marked with a red asterisk are mandatory).
Accept the Assurance/declaration by selecting "Yes," then click "Save" to complete the application and generate the Application Number.
Upload your photograph and signature in JPG format (photo: 5 cm height x 3.6 cm width; signature: 2.5 cm height x 7.5 cm width; each file up to 15 KB).
Click "Confirm Application," enter the Application Number and Birth Date, review all details carefully, and confirm. Note that no changes are possible after confirmation.
Click "Print Application" to download and save a copy of the confirmed application form.
Pay the examination fee online through the "Print Application Form / Pay Fees" option before the fee payment deadline, and save the payment receipt.
important_instructions: Only one application should be submitted per candidate; if multiple applications are submitted, only the last confirmed application will be considered valid. No changes can be made to the category (caste) details or any other information after the application is confirmed. Candidates must keep their original certificates ready, as details in the application must match these certificates exactly; discrepancies can lead to rejection at any stage. The same photograph uploaded during application must be used throughout the recruitment process, including at the exam center and during document verification. Keep the mobile number and email ID provided in the application active until the recruitment process is complete, as important updates will be sent through SMS/email. Candidates found using unfair means, submitting false information, or violating the Gujarat Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2023 will face disqualification and legal action.

rows.details: Gujarat Maritime Board (GMB), under the Ports and Transport Department

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Online Application Start Date: 15 July 2026 (2:00 PM) Online Application Last Date: 29 July 2026 (11:59 PM) Last Date to Pay Examination Fee: 1 August 2026 (11:59 PM) Reference Date for Age, Educational Qualification and Other Eligibility: 29 July 2026 Probable Qualifying Test (Shorthand Test) Month: September 2026 (tentative, as per the notification) The exact examination date, time and admit card download instructions will be published later on the GSSSB website. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official website regularly for updates.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The candidate must be a citizen of India or possess the nationality prescribed under Rule-7 of the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. A bachelor's degree from any university established or incorporated by or under a Central or State Act in India, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Government. Basic knowledge of computer applications as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi, or both. Candidates who do not currently hold a certificate for basic computer knowledge can still apply, but they must produce such a certificate before appointment; otherwise they will not be eligible for appointment. As on 29 July 2026, the candidate's age must not be less than 20 years and not more than 40 years. The upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates already in Gujarat Government service, as per the applicable rules. Age relaxation for other categories is as follows:

content_sections.heading: Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be appointed on a fixed monthly pay of Rs. 40,800/- for the first five years, along with other benefits admissible under the relevant Finance Department resolutions. On satisfactory completion of five years of service, the candidate becomes eligible for regular appointment in the 7th Pay Commission pay scale of Rs. 35,400/- to Rs. 1,12,400/- (Level-6), subject to the outcome of SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012 pending before the Supreme Court.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The fee must be paid only online through Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI or Wallet, on or before 1 August 2026 (11:59 PM). A transaction fee will apply in addition to the examination fee. The fee paid is refundable only to those candidates who actually appear for the examination; it will not be refunded to candidates who do not appear.

rows.category: Reserved Category (Women of all categories, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, and Ex-servicemen)

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for the English Stenographer, Grade-2 post consists of the following stages: Dictation of an English passage in shorthand at a speed of 80 words per minute for two minutes, followed by transcription at a speed of 7 words per minute (in legible handwriting, not typing). This test checks the candidate's shorthand-writing ability; only candidates who meet the required skill level are declared eligible for the Main Examination. Paper-1: English Language Proficiency and Computer Knowledge (Descriptive and Objective) - 100 Marks, 2 Hours The Basic Computer Knowledge portion (20 marks) covers computer basics, software (MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint, MS Outlook, Windows, Indic), working with internet and email, and basics of networking and cyber security. Paper-2: Skill Test (Shorthand and Transcription Typing) - 100 Marks Dictation of 2 passages of 4 minutes each (8 minutes total) at a shorthand speed of 100 words per minute in English. Transcription/typing time: 50 minutes, done on a computer. For every 3 errors in transcription, 1 mark will be deducted from the allotted marks. Minimum qualifying marks for Paper-2 is 50; marks below 50 are treated as zero. The minimum qualifying marks for Paper-1 is 40 and for Paper-2 is 50. The final merit list is prepared out of the total 200 marks from both papers, and there will be no waiting list for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Educational qualification certificates Birth date/age proof certificate School Leaving Certificate Category certificate (SC/ST/SEBC/EWS), where applicable Non-creamy layer certificate (for SEBC candidates), where applicable Disability certificate (for PwD candidates), where applicable Ex-servicemen certificate/discharge book, where applicable Basic computer knowledge certificate Passport-size photograph (white background, recent) and signature, in the prescribed format for upload

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Only one application should be submitted per candidate; if multiple applications are submitted, only the last confirmed application will be considered valid. No changes can be made to the category (caste) details or any other information after the application is confirmed. Candidates must keep their original certificates ready, as details in the application must match these certificates exactly; discrepancies can lead to rejection at any stage. The same photograph uploaded during application must be used throughout the recruitment process, including at the exam center and during document verification. Keep the mobile number and email ID provided in the application active until the recruitment process is complete, as important updates will be sent through SMS/email. Candidates found using unfair means, submitting false information, or violating the Gujarat Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2023 will face disqualification and legal action.

faq.question: Q1. What is GSSSB English Stenographer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment drive by the Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) to fill 1 vacancy of English Stenographer, Grade-2, Class-3 under the Gujarat Maritime Board, through Advertisement No. 436/2026-27.

faq.question: Q2. When can I apply online for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Online applications can be submitted from 15 July 2026 (2:00 PM) to 29 July 2026 (11:59 PM) through the OJAS website.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this post?
faq.answer: As on 29 July 2026, candidates must be between 20 and 40 years of age, with age relaxation available for certain categories as detailed in the notification.

faq.question: Q4. What is the pay scale for English Stenographer, Grade-2?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will get a fixed pay of Rs. 40,800/- per month for the first five years, followed by the regular Level-6 pay scale of Rs. 35,400/- to Rs. 1,12,400/- upon satisfactory completion of service, subject to pending Supreme Court proceedings.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Qualifying Test (Shorthand Test), followed by a Main Examination consisting of Paper-1 (Descriptive and Objective) and Paper-2 (Skill Test in shorthand and transcription typing).

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 500/- for unreserved category candidates and Rs. 400/- for reserved category candidates (women of all categories, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, and ex-servicemen), refundable to those who appear for the exam.

faq.question: Q7. Is there a waiting list for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No. As per the notification, there will be no waiting list for this advertisement; vacancies remaining unfilled will be carried forward to a future recruitment drive.
